prompt: A Practicum Assessment & Access Governance system with main features including secure local accounts, multi-role access control for student, faculty advisor, corporate mentor, and department admin users, and a full assignment and quiz assessment workflow for internship cohorts. Users interact through an English web interface built with HTMX-enhanced server-rendered screens that provide fast, inline feedback for actions like logging in, switching roles, launching a timed quiz, saving answers, and reviewing graded results without full page reloads. Department admins configure schools, majors, classes, and internship cohorts, then publish assessments by assembling papers from a question bank that supports single choice, multiple choice, true/false, fill-in, and short answer. For each paper, admins set a time limit (default 45 minutes), attempt limits (default 1 attempt, max 3), an availability window (e.g., 03/25/2026 9:00 AM–5:00 PM), and randomization rules (e.g., draw 20 questions from a tagged pool with per-student shuffling). Students see a clear countdown timer, autosave indicators every 15 seconds, and hard duplicate-submission prevention that blocks resubmits after a successful finalization; faculty advisors and corporate mentors can view only the cohorts they are assigned to and perform manual grading on subjective responses with comment threads and rubric guidance. Score summaries, item difficulty breakdowns, and cohort comparisons are viewable on-screen and exportable to CSV for offline reporting. The backend uses Flask to enforce authorization, validation, and auditing, with SQLite as the local persistence layer so the system runs fully offline on a single machine or an isolated on-prem network with no third-party dependencies. Authentication is strictly username and password with salted-hash storage, password strength rules requiring at least 12 characters with three of four character classes, CAPTCHA after 3 failed attempts, and account lockout for 15 minutes after 8 failures; sessions expire after 30 minutes of inactivity and require re-auth for high-risk actions like role changes and permission edits. Authorization follows RBAC with data-scope constraints (self, own department, sub-departments, or global; plus school/major/class/cohort scopes) applied consistently to both menus and API endpoints, and supports permission templates and temporary delegations that expire automatically (default 7 days, max 30). Sensitive fields such as student ID are masked in most views and encrypted at rest using a locally stored key; optional offline MFA can be enabled via TOTP codes generated by an authenticator app without any network calls. Every critical action is written to immutable audit logs capturing who performed it, when, what changed, and where it originated (device/browser fingerprint and local IP), with searchable filters and exports, retention defaults to 3 years, and anomalous login patterns flagged on an admin dashboard for review. Frontend requests and backend processing are separated through REST-style endpoints that return HTML fragments for HTMX swaps, ensuring consistent permission checks and audit capture on every state-changing operation.
